design.summary:
M18 (FINAL milestone) is the self-improving-pillar capstone: an agent extends its own T4 skill library on TABOS under a FROZEN-KERNEL / EVOLVING-USERSPACE split, where the held-out evaluator + test set live in a kernel domain the agent PROVABLY cannot read or write. The whole safety guarantee REDUCES TO the already-shipped M11 rights-mask invariant — no new mechanism, ZERO new unsafe. It is purely additive bodies + facades over a frozen, closed ABI.

VERIFIED AGAINST SOURCE (all recon claims hold):
- M_MEM_WRITE_PROC=18 (caps.rs) is wired into required_right()->Rights::WRITE_PROCEDURAL (1<<7) but has NO dispatch match arm — it passes the rights gate then falls to `_ => SysReturn::ok(0)` (the catch-all, last line of dispatch). It is a reserved-but-unbodied stub exactly as its doc-comment says ("the privileged T4 procedural write ... lands M18"). M18 FILLS this arm.
- The M11 dispatch chokepoint resolves args.handle ONLY against the caller's table (table.live(h)): out-of-range slot -> BadCap; vacant/wrong-generation -> Stale (occupancy check independent of generation match, so a forged u64 cannot manufacture authority). Then `if !table.slots[i].rights.contains(need) { return Denied }`. Authority lives in the SLOT, not the handle.
- ObjKind::Skill already exists at discriminant 6 (Agent=0,ModelSession=1,MemoryHome=2,MemoryRecord=3,Budget=4,Channel=5,Skill=6,Namespace=7,Generic=8,Block=9); ObjKind is append-only.
- The born-with home in agent_spawn (lib.rs) carries mh_rights = READ|WRITE|RECALL — NOT WRITE_PROCEDURAL — so an ordinary agent's M_MEM_WRITE_PROC is already Denied. The live M11 self-test at main.rs:~1156 already witnesses this (M_MEM_WRITE_PROC on a READ-only home -> Denied).
- mem.rs already has the EvolveR utility s=(c_succ+1)*SCALE/(c_use+2) (in forget_sweep), MemRecord.c_succ/c_use, the push_record discipline, the TOKEN_QUOTA write-amplification cap, SCALE=1000 fixed-point determinism, and the op-selector-inside-one-method precedent (M17 widened consolidate(op,a,b,c) to op 0..8 with NO new ABI). All forbid-unsafe.

The frozen boundary is realized by making the evaluator/test-set/lineage UNNAMEABLE (no handle in any agent table) and UNWRITABLE (no WRITE_PROCEDURAL toward them, monotonic attenuation can never amplify) — so Goodharting and test-set peeking are capability-Denied operations, unrepresentable rather than merely discouraged. This is strictly stronger than DGM (which only CAUGHT node-114 detector-removal post-hoc via lineage) and STOP (which could only MEASURE sandbox escapes).
design.frozen_boundary:
FROZEN BOUNDARY == THE M11 RIGHTS-MASK INVARIANT, VERBATIM. No new boundary mechanism is built; caps.rs already states (module header) "M18's frozen boundary reduces to it." Realized by TWO independent, redundant gates already enforced by caps::dispatch:

GATE 1 — NO-HANDLE (primary, the measurer/measured separation). The Evaluator + held-out test set + append-only lineage log are minted into a KERNEL-OWNED HandleTable + MemSubstrate (call it `eval_tbl` / `eval_home`) that is NEVER inserted into any agent's table and appears in no derivation/attenuation closure of any cap the improving agent holds. caps::dispatch resolves args.handle ONLY against the CALLER's table via table.live(h). Therefore the improving agent literally cannot NAME eval_home: any handle it presents either resolves to its OWN object (never the evaluator) or is a fabricated raw u64 -> BadCap (slot out of range) / Stale (vacant or wrong generation; the occupancy check is independent of the generation match, so a forged generation that happens to equal a vacant slot's still fails). Authority lives in the SLOT (caps.rs Handle docs), and the kernel simply never places that slot in any agent table. This is the modern, kernel-enforced form of Eurisko's hand-rolled "protected section the heuristics cannot modify" and GenProg's "deleted trusted-output.txt" fix.

GATE 2 — NO-RIGHT (defense in depth). Even if a handle to eval_home leaked, the agent's static AgentManifest (the seL4 initial-CNode analogue, lib.rs) declares no evaluator grant, and mint_manifest_caps mints only declared CapGrants; the evaluator's MemSubstrate is not WRITE_PROCEDURAL-granted to the agent. A touch hits the dispatch gate `if !table.slots[i].rights.contains(need) { return Denied }`. Monotonic attenuation (Rights::intersect is the ONLY rights-changing primitive) means no narrow/dup/transfer chain can ever amplify into the evaluator's authority.

NET: a visible metric gets Goodharted, so the evaluator is made UNVISIBLE by capability geometry — the agent provably cannot READ the held-out set (no READ-righted handle to eval_home -> Denied/BadCap; no peeking/overfitting, closing the METR "return the reference answer" attack) nor WRITE the evaluator/lineage (no WRITE/WRITE_PROCEDURAL handle -> Denied/BadCap; no Goodharting, closing the DGM node-114 / GenProg attacks). Goodharting is unrepresentable in the agent's authority.

LOAD-BEARING CAVEAT (ROADMAP-V2.md:294): M18 can print its marker and STILL be unsafe if a confused-deputy bug in M11 lets an agent reach an evaluator. So the M11 chokepoint must carry the Kani/Verus proof (no {mint,dup,narrow,transfer,revoke} sequence yields a handle/right to a frozen-domain object outside an explicit grant; rights of any resolvable handle are a subset of the cap it derived from). The marker proves liveness; the proof proves the safety property. Treat any M11 confused-deputy/revocation gap as an M18 BLOCKER.
design.skill_tier:
T4 PROCEDURAL/SKILL TIER — stored by EXTENDING crates/tb-hal/src/mem.rs (keeps line-1 #![forbid(unsafe_code)]). Add a fifth tier to the substrate. ALL fixed-point integer, deterministic/replayable, ZERO unsafe.

1) NEW FIELD on MemSubstrate (mem.rs, alongside t0..t3): `t4: ProceduralStore`, initialized in MemSubstrate::new() as empty.

2) NEW TYPES (mem.rs, pure safe alloc):
   struct SkillRecord {
       id: u64,
       body_tok: u64,     // executable/WASM-component token (inline scalar at M18, like every other tier)
       desc_tok: u64,     // NL description token (Voyager: embedding-indexed retrieval seam)
       iface_tok: u64,    // WIT-typed interface token
       embedding: u64,    // description embedding (top-k retrieval, future)
       c_succ: u32, c_use: u32,   // EvolveR utility counters — start at 0 (skill earns trust)
       util: u32,         // ACT-R production-compilation utility (starts 0; U += alpha(R-U), alpha=0.2 -> fixed-point U += (R-U)/5)
       lineage: Vec<u64>, // parent ids / provenance (DGM archive lineage)
       tier: u8,          // PROPOSED(0) vs ADMITTED(1) — shadow/canary state
       provenance: u8,
   }
   struct ProceduralStore { skills: Vec<SkillRecord>, next_id: u64 }

3) WRITE PATH (the agent verb): a new `pub(crate) fn write_proc(&mut self, op: u64, a: u64, b: u64, c: u64) -> Option<u64>` on MemSubstrate, with an OP-SELECTOR inside (NO new ABI method — the M17 precedent): op=0 ADD_SKILL (push a PROPOSED SkillRecord, util 0, body_tok=a, desc_tok=b, iface/embedding packed in c), op=1 UPDATE_UTILITY, op=2 READ_SKILL (returns body_tok of the agent's OWN skill), op=3 LINK_LINEAGE. Reuse the existing push_record discipline: enforce the TOKEN_QUOTA write-amplification cap so a flood of PROPOSED skills fails-closed (returns None -> NoMem), and bump self.clock. A PROPOSED record is INERT (util 0, tier=PROPOSED) — it never beats the deliberative path — until the harness flips it to ADMITTED (verification-before-commit, SELF-IMPROVEMENT-SPEC s5).

4) HARNESS-ONLY (kernel-side) methods on MemSubstrate, called by lib.rs facades (never reachable from an agent's M11 dispatch because they are not method-numbered): `skill_get(id) -> Option<(u64 /*body_tok*/, u8 /*tier*/)>`, `skill_admit(id, reward) -> bool` (flips PROPOSED->ADMITTED, applies U += (reward-U)/5, appends lineage), `skill_count_admitted()`.

RISK ASYMMETRY (CoALA, preserved): a normal agent's born-with home carries READ|WRITE|RECALL only — NOT WRITE_PROCEDURAL — so an ordinary agent writing a skill is Denied at the chokepoint (already witnessed by the live M11 test). Only a home minted WITH WRITE_PROCEDURAL (granted kernel-mediated by the harness facade to the forked self-improver, mirroring how agent_model_open grants INVOKE_MODEL the manifest never declared) can propose. The skill home is a SEPARATE MemoryHome object from the born-with episodic home (rights can only be narrowed, never amplified, so an existing home cannot gain WRITE_PROCEDURAL).
design.held_out_evaluator:
HELD-OUT EVALUATOR — a kernel-side DETERMINISTIC scorer over a held-out test set the agent cannot read. Lives in the FROZEN domain (Gate 1): a kernel-owned `eval_tbl: HandleTable` holding `eval_home = eval_tbl.mint_memory_home(READ|WRITE|RECALL)` seeded with held-out (input_tok -> expected_tok) records — eval_home is NEVER minted into any agent table and no facade ever takes or returns it.

SCORING MODEL (fully deterministic, no_std, fixed-point, no floats — the same SCALE=1000 integer discipline as mem.rs recall):
- A skill's behavior is modeled by a deterministic transform f(body_tok, input_tok) (e.g. a fixed-point hash/rotate transform like the M17 reflect_digest seed). The "correct" generalizing body_tok is what the evaluator is secretly looking for; the agent does not know it.
- Held-out test set = a set of (input_tok, expected_tok) pairs stored in eval_home that the agent has NO handle to (so it cannot tune to them — no peeking/overfitting).
- score(skill) = count of held-out cases where f(skill.body_tok, input) == expected, normalized to [0, SCALE].
- The agent may see a small PUBLIC/visible training slice (in its own t4 / a public token), but the held-out slice is fresh and unreadable, so a skill that games the visible examples scores low on held-out.

A `pub(crate) fn score_candidate(&self, body_tok: u64) -> u32` on the evaluator's MemSubstrate runs f over its held-out records and returns the fixed-point score. The evaluator executes IN THE FROZEN DOMAIN (kernel-side, over eval_tbl), NEVER inside the candidate's sandbox — the candidate cannot introspect or rewrite the scoring code (the recon "validate, not just hide" sharpening).

ANTI-CROSS-GENERATION-GOODHART (SELF-IMPROVEMENT-SPEC s9, recommended): default to MULTIPLE held-out evaluators and reserve a never-exposed partition sampled per admission, so accumulated overfitting on any visible-but-held-out slice still fails the never-exposed slice. At M18 a single deterministic held-out set is the DoD floor; the rotating partition is a documented forward hook.
design.harness_admission:
HARNESS — a KERNEL-OWNED fork->modify->validate->merge pipeline implementing the three-laws ABI precedence (SELF-IMPROVEMENT-SPEC s0/s8: ENDURE > EXCEL > EVOLVE; a lower rung never overrides a higher), built as kernel-side facades in lib.rs (`agent_skill_propose`, `agent_evolve_request`) on the EXISTING agent_model_open / agent_block_create kernel-mediated precedent — kernel-mediated precisely because they must reach the kernel-owned eval_tbl that caps::dispatch (holding only &mut HandleTable) cannot.

ADMISSION RULE (propose -> frozen evaluator scores on held-out -> admit ONLY if improved):
1) PROPOSE. The forked self-improving agent (an M12 agent_spawn into a default-deny quota'd sandbox) writes a candidate SkillRecord into ITS OWN t4 via M_MEM_WRITE_PROC (WRITE_PROCEDURAL-gated, kernel-mediated grant). Record lands tier=PROPOSED, util 0 (inert).
2) ENDURE (safety). The kernel runs the held-out SAFETY suite (records in eval_home) against the candidate; any EMIT_EXTERNAL-tagged / high-impact skill trips the mandatory human-approval hook (documented as a deferred stub for the disarmed-timer self-test, like M14's deferred copy_to_user). Fail -> reject, append to lineage, STOP (lower rung never overridden).
3) EXCEL (regression / no peeking). The FROZEN evaluator scores the candidate on the held-out test set via score_candidate(body_tok) using its kernel-private fixed-point rule. Admit ONLY if the score STRICTLY improves on the current best (no regression). The agent never sees the inputs NOR the threshold, so a skill that games a visible counter does not move the held-out number -> rejected. This is where a Goodharting/overfit proposal dies.
4) EVOLVE (merge). On pass, the kernel flips the agent's SkillRecord PROPOSED->ADMITTED (skill_admit: util U += (R-U)/5) and appends to the immutable lineage log; rollback = snapshot restore. ONLY the agent's own subtree is mutated; the kernel/evaluator/lineage stay frozen.

The validation result is an UNFORGEABLE kernel verdict, not a log the agent produced — directly closing the DGM "faked the unit-test log" failure (the agent can't write the lineage either, no handle). The whole safety guarantee reduces to the M11 rights mask. DoD marker emitted by the kernel after the self-test block: "M18: evolve OK".
design.abi:
REUSE-ONLY. The closed numbered method set [0..31] stays FROZEN — NO new method numbers (preserving the M16/M14 "unknown method == BadMethod" proofs at main.rs and the deliberately-sparse-but-closed design). M18 adds only BODIES + kernel facades — the framekernel dividend.

1) AGENT-FACING SKILL WRITE: reuse M_MEM_WRITE_PROC=18, gated by Rights::WRITE_PROCEDURAL (1<<7). It is already wired into required_right() but currently UNBODIED (verified: zero match arms in dispatch -> falls to `_ => SysReturn::ok(0)`). M18 adds ONE dispatch match arm for M_MEM_WRITE_PROC that, after the existing WRITE_PROCEDURAL gate, clones the Rc<Object> out (drop the slot borrow first), matches obj.mem, and calls cell.borrow_mut().write_proc(args.args[0], args.args[1], args.args[2], args.args[3]) — the EXACT clone-Rc-out / drop-slot-borrow-before-minting discipline of the existing M_MEM_WRITE arm. The procedural verbs (ADD_SKILL / UPDATE_UTILITY / READ_SKILL / LINK_LINEAGE) ride an OP-SELECTOR in args[0] — the precise pattern M17 used to add distill/reflect/forget inside M_MEM_CONSOLIDATE without new ABI.

2) HARNESS / EVOLVE CONTROL PLANE: NOT a dispatch method (it needs the kernel-owned eval_tbl + the AddressSpace for the sandbox fork, which dispatch cannot reach holding only &mut HandleTable). It rides kernel-side facades in lib.rs — `agent_skill_propose(task, op, a0, a1, a2)` and `agent_evolve_request(task) -> (status, admitted_bool)` — exactly as agent_model_open / agent_block_create / agent_channel_connect do their kernel-mediated minting. No new syscall number, no new EL0 gate.

3) OBJECTS: reuse ObjKind::Skill (already defined, discriminant 6) if a per-skill capability object is wanted; or — simpler — keep skills as id-addressed records INSIDE the MemoryHome substrate (the MemoryRecord precedent), addressed via the op-selector. The EvaluatorHome reuses ObjKind::MemoryHome. ObjKind is append-only, so no discriminant perturbation. M18 needs NO new ObjKind.

4) RIGHTS: reuse WRITE_PROCEDURAL (1<<7) for skill writes; CONSOLIDATE (1<<9) for harness maintenance. The evaluator needs NO right (it is unreachable by construction). No new Rights bit.

5) SysStatus: reuse Ok / Denied / BadCap / Stale / NoMem. No new status.

Result: the closed method space, closed ObjKind set, closed Rights set, and closed SysStatus enum all stay FROZEN; M18 is purely additive bodies + facades.
design.module_location:
All safe-Rust; the only file touched that is not already #![forbid(unsafe_code)] is main.rs, and it adds NO unsafe.

1) crates/tb-hal/src/caps.rs (#![forbid(unsafe_code)]): add ONE dispatch match arm for M_MEM_WRITE_PROC (currently unbodied, falls to `_ => SysReturn::ok(0)`). After the existing WRITE_PROCEDURAL gate, clone the Rc<Object> out, match &obj.mem, call cell.borrow_mut().write_proc(args.args[0..3]); Some(id) -> ok(id), None -> err(NoMem). No new method number, no new Rights, ObjKind::Skill already present. Mirrors the M_MEM_WRITE arm byte-for-byte in structure.

2) crates/tb-hal/src/mem.rs (#![forbid(unsafe_code)]): add `t4: ProceduralStore` field to MemSubstrate + init in new(); add `struct SkillRecord{...}` + `struct ProceduralStore{ skills, next_id }`; add `pub(crate) fn write_proc(&mut self, op,a,b,c) -> Option<u64>` (op-selector add/update/read/link, reusing push_record's TOKEN_QUOTA + clock discipline and the EvolveR utility (c_succ+1)*SCALE/(c_use+2)); add harness-only `pub(crate) fn skill_get/skill_admit/skill_count_admitted` and the evaluator's `pub(crate) fn score_candidate(&self, body_tok) -> u32`. All fixed-point integer, deterministic, ZERO unsafe.

3) crates/tb-hal/src/lib.rs: add kernel-mediated harness facades `agent_skill_propose` / `agent_evolve_request` (the agent_model_open / agent_block_create precedent) that (a) kernel-mediated-grant a WRITE_PROCEDURAL skill-home into the forked self-improver's table (like agent_model_open grants INVOKE_MODEL), (b) build the kernel-owned eval_tbl/eval_home, (c) run ENDURE->EXCEL scoring over held-out records, (d) flip the agent's t4 record PROPOSED->ADMITTED on strict improvement. Uses the existing blessed AGENTS UnsafeCell registry (single-core, interrupts masked) — NO new unsafe. Never returns or accepts the eval_home handle.

4) kernel/src/main.rs: new M18 self-test block inserted AFTER the `M17: consolidate OK` marker print (main.rs:~2196) and BEFORE the final tb_hal::halt() (main.rs:~2199); add `static STACK_AGENT_E: TaskStack<4096>` + `static MANIFEST_E` (the self-improver). Emit "M18: evolve OK". Adds NO unsafe.

REUSED UNCHANGED: caps::dispatch chokepoint, HandleTable.live/mint/mint_memory_home, MemSubstrate tiers, the AgentManifest/CapGrant least-privilege path, mint_manifest_caps, M10 address-space isolation, the M16 mock model bridge (available as the skill-distill seam). DOC TO UPDATE: ROADMAP-V2.md:385 M18 status row -> complete.
design.selftest:
New M18 self-test block in kernel/src/main.rs, inserted AFTER the `M17: consolidate OK` print (~2196) and BEFORE the final tb_hal::halt() (~2199), following the M13/M17 WITNESS idiom (kernel-side over caps::dispatch with a local HandleTable; timer disarmed since pre-M9, so single-core/interrupts-masked — the RefCell discipline holds). Add a local `m18_fail(why)->!` mirroring m13_fail/m17_fail, and reuse the existing `disp(tbl,h,method,[u64;4])` helper. Add `static STACK_AGENT_E` + `static MANIFEST_E`; spawn agent_e via agent_spawn.

SETUP: kernel-owned `let mut eval_tbl = caps::HandleTable::with_capacity(16); let eval_home = eval_tbl.mint_memory_home(READ|WRITE|RECALL);` seeded with held-out (input,expected) records (these define the secret target body_tok). Agent-side `let mut tbl = caps::HandleTable::with_capacity(16); let home_wp = tbl.mint_memory_home(READ|WRITE|RECALL|WRITE_PROCEDURAL);` (the harness-granted skill home) and `let home_ro = tbl.mint_memory_home(READ|WRITE|RECALL);` (an ordinary born-with home, no WRITE_PROCEDURAL).

ASSERTS (concrete):
(1) GOOD SKILL ADMITTED: `let p = disp(&mut tbl, home_wp, M_MEM_WRITE_PROC, [OP_ADD, BODY_GOOD, DESC, IFACE]); assert p.status==Ok;` (proves WRITE_PROCEDURAL bites POSITIVELY on a granted home). Then harness scores BODY_GOOD over eval_home -> strictly improves -> skill_admit. Assert the record reads back tier==ADMITTED via disp(home_wp, M_MEM_WRITE_PROC, [OP_READ, id,..]) and lineage grew.
(2) BAD/GOODHARTING SKILL REJECTED: propose BODY_OVERFIT that games the visible counter but does not match the held-out set -> score not strictly-better -> harness leaves it PROPOSED (util 0, inert). Assert it is NOT admitted (skill_count_admitted unchanged) and was appended to lineage as rejected.
(3) PROVABLY CANNOT READ THE HELD-OUT SET: no handle the agent holds resolves eval_home (it lives only in eval_tbl, never minted into tbl). Assert a fabricated raw handle against tbl -> BadCap/Stale, e.g. `assert disp(&mut tbl, Handle::from_raw(0x0000_0001_0000_00FF), M_MEM_READ, [0;4]).status in {BadCap,Stale}`; and that no agent_* facade exposes eval_tbl (compile-time: none takes it).
(4) PROVABLY CANNOT WRITE THE EVALUATOR (Goodhart-stop): reuse the live M11 denial idiom (main.rs:~1156) — `assert disp(&mut tbl, home_ro, M_MEM_WRITE_PROC, [OP_ADD,..]).status==Denied` (a home WITHOUT WRITE_PROCEDURAL); and any attempt to reach eval_home returns BadCap/Stale (no handle). Both are the rights-mask invariant.
(5) CLOSED-SET INTACT: `assert disp(&mut tbl, home_wp, 0xDEAD, [0;4]).status==BadMethod` (the M11/M16 closed-set proof untouched).
WITNESS B (through a spawned agent): drive agent_skill_propose(agent_e, OP_ADD, BODY_GOOD,..) -> Ok then agent_evolve_request(agent_e) -> (Ok, admitted=true); and agent_e proposing via its ordinary born-with home -> Denied — proving the per-agent guarantee end-to-end through the M11 chokepoint, not a kernel-local toy.

Then emit: "mem: skill proposed via WRITE_PROCEDURAL, frozen evaluator admitted-good + rejected-bad, held-out set unreadable + evaluator unwritable (Denied/BadCap)\n" and the DoD marker "M18: evolve OK\n". All prior M2..M17 markers still print (cumulative). ZERO new unsafe in the test (pure safe dispatch + facade calls).
design.new_unsafe: ZERO new unsafe — CONFIRMED and machine-enforced. The three library files extended (caps.rs, mem.rs are #![forbid(unsafe_code)] at line 1; lib.rs's new facades add only safe code over the EXISTING blessed AGENTS UnsafeCell registry — no new unsafe block) and the main.rs self-test adds no unsafe. The skill store, the write_proc op-selector, the held-out evaluator/scorer (fixed-point integer), and the harness facades are all 100% safe Rust. The ENTIRE frozen guarantee is the EXISTING caps gate — no new privileged path beyond the already-reserved M_MEM_WRITE_PROC=18 / WRITE_PROCEDURAL. The forbid-unsafe attribute on caps.rs and mem.rs makes "zero new unsafe" a COMPILE-TIME invariant, not a review promise. The only pre-existing unsafe in tb-hal (the per-arch register-lift shim in arch::*::user, the TaskStack/AGENTS UnsafeCell) is untouched and unextended.
risks: CONFUSED-DEPUTY / node-114 (the headline risk): the ENTIRE M18 safety guarantee reduces to the M11 rights-mask invariant, so a single confused-deputy bug in caps::dispatch (resolving a handle against the wrong table, or a rights-check that reads the handle's bits instead of the slot's) would let an agent reach an evaluator and Goodhart it. ROADMAP-V2.md:294 is explicit. MITIGATION: the M11 chokepoint must carry the Kani/Verus proof (a proof, not just a marker); authority already lives in the slot, not the handle. Treat any M11 confused-deputy/revocation gap as an M18 BLOCKER, not an M11 nicety.

VISIBLE-METRIC GOODHARTING: if ANY part of the held-out score or test inputs becomes agent-readable (a leaked READ handle, an instrumentation marker the agent can write, or a facade that returns eval records), the metric is gamed. MITIGATION: no agent_* facade may take or return the eval_home handle; the manifest omits any evaluator grant; default to multiple held-out evaluators + a never-exposed rotating partition so no single visible metric exists; belt-and-suspenders agent-unmapped address region (M10). Skalse proves no metric is intrinsically unhackable, so the BOUNDARY (not the metric) carries the guarantee.

CLOSED-SET DRIFT: adding a new method number or a new ObjKind discriminant in the wrong position would break the M16/M14 'unknown method == BadMethod' proofs and any #[repr(u32)] consumer of M_OBJECT_INSPECT. MITIGATION: reuse M_MEM_WRITE_PROC=18 with an internal op-selector (the M17 precedent), append-only ObjKind, reuse ObjKind::MemoryHome for the evaluator and ObjKind::Skill (already defined) if a per-skill object is wanted.

REFCELL RE-ENTRANCY: the new M_MEM_WRITE_PROC arm reaches obj.mem.borrow_mut() (write_proc) while a slot reference is in scope — a reentrant borrow would panic. MITIGATION: follow the established clone-Rc-out / drop-slot-borrow-BEFORE-borrow_mut discipline of the M_MEM_WRITE and M_MEM_RECALL arms; single-core, interrupts masked.

QUOTA / DoS: an agent could spam PROPOSED skills to OOM the T4 store. MITIGATION: reuse the existing TOKEN_QUOTA write-amplification cap (KeyKOS space-bank) in write_proc so proposals fail-closed (None -> NoMem) past the bound; PROPOSED records stay inert (util 0) until admitted.

TRUST-PROMOTION BYPASS: a learned skill must NOT beat the deliberative path before earning trust. MITIGATION: SkillRecord starts util 0 / tier PROPOSED (shadow/canary); only the harness (after a held-out EXCEL pass) flips it ADMITTED via skill_admit with U += (R-U)/5 — never the agent itself; verification-before-commit is structural, not advisory.

GRANT-PATH LEAK: the WRITE_PROCEDURAL skill-home must be a SEPARATE object minted kernel-mediated into ONLY the forked self-improver (the agent_model_open INVOKE_MODEL precedent) — never added to the public manifest and never minted on the born-with episodic home (rights can only be narrowed, so an existing home can never gain WRITE_PROCEDURAL anyway). MITIGATION: the facade mints a distinct skill-home; the ordinary born-with home stays READ|WRITE|RECALL, so any normal-agent skill write is Denied (the live M11 idiom).

DEFERRED HUMAN-APPROVAL HOOK: the mandatory human-approval gate on high-impact / EMIT_EXTERNAL skills (SELF-IMPROVEMENT-SPEC s8) cannot be exercised by a disarmed-timer kernel self-test; it is a documented stub at M18 (like M14's deferred copy_to_user / blocking-recv and M17's BULK daemon task). The structural capability boundary (no handle to evaluator/lineage) holds regardless.

PROOF-vs-LIVENESS GAP: the on-target 'M18: evolve OK' marker proves only liveness; the rights-mask correctness (attenuation monotonicity, the no-handle invariant) must be covered by host cargo test / Kani on caps.rs, per the ROADMAP no-heap->heap / proof-not-marker guidance.

recon.frozen_boundary:
The M18 frozen boundary IS the M11 rights-mask invariant verbatim, not a new mechanism. caps.rs:33-40 already states it: "THE INVARIANT this module is a PROOF of (M18's frozen boundary reduces to it): across every sequence of {mint,dup,narrow,transfer,revoke}, the rights of any handle a principal can resolve are a SUBSET of the rights of the capability it was derived from ... AND dispatch/resolve returns Ok ONLY for a handle whose generation equals the live, OCCUPIED slot." ROADMAP-V2.md:279-296 confirms the M18 guarantee "reduces to the M11 rights-mask invariant + M10/M12 isolation."

CONCRETE REALIZATION (two independent, redundant gates, both already enforced by caps::dispatch):
1) NO-HANDLE (primary). caps::dispatch (caps.rs:~960) resolves args.handle ONLY against the CALLER's table via table.live(h) (caps.rs:~560): out-of-range slot -> BadCap; vacant/wrong-generation slot -> Stale (occupancy check is INDEPENDENT of the generation match, so a forged u64 cannot manufacture authority). The evaluator + held-out test set + lineage log live in a kernel-OWNED HandleTable/MemSubstrate whose handle is NEVER minted into any agent table. The improving agent literally cannot NAME them: any read/write attempt either presents a handle the agent does hold (resolves to the agent's OWN object, never the evaluator) or a fabricated raw u64 (-> BadCap/Stale). Authority lives in the SLOT, not the handle (caps.rs:24-31), and the kernel simply never places that slot in the agent's table - the exact realization the prompt names.
2) NO-RIGHT (defense in depth). Even if a handle leaked, the agent's static AgentManifest (lib.rs:1182, the seL4 initial-CNode analogue) omits any evaluator grant, and mint_manifest_caps (lib.rs:1254) mints only declared CapGrants. A touch then hits the dispatch rights gate `if !table.slots[i].rights.contains(need) { return Denied }` (caps.rs:~975). This is the SAME path the live M11 self-test already exercises at main.rs:1156 (M_MEM_WRITE_PROC on a READ-only handle -> Denied) and the M12 AGENT_DENIED_OK witness (lib.rs:1248). Monotonic attenuation (Rights::intersect, the ONLY rights-changing primitive, caps.rs:~170) means no narrow/dup/transfer chain can ever amplify into the evaluator's authority.

Net: a visible metric gets Goodharted, so the evaluator is made UNVISIBLE by capability geometry - the agent provably cannot read the held-out set (no READ-righted handle -> Denied/BadCap) nor write the evaluator/lineage (no WRITE handle -> Denied/BadCap). This is the DGM measurer/measured separation expressed purely as the M11 chokepoint, ZERO new mechanism.
recon.skill_tier:
T4 PROCEDURAL/SKILL tier, stored by EXTENDING crates/tb-hal/src/mem.rs (line-1 #![forbid(unsafe_code)], stays machine-enforced safe). The substrate already has T0-T3 (mem.rs:330 MemSubstrate{t0,t1,t2,t3,finsts,clock,quota,backing,imp_accum,...}); M18 adds a fifth tier field `t4: ProceduralStore` holding `Vec<SkillRecord>`.

SkillRecord (per SELF-IMPROVEMENT-SPEC.md s5 + MEMORY-SPEC.md:24-25 "T4 PROCEDURAL/SKILL: executable skills + distilled principles; write is privileged"): { id:u64, body_tok:u64 (the WASM-component / executable code token, inline-scalar at M18 like every other tier), desc_tok:u64 (NL description), iface_tok:u64 (WIT-typed interface), embedding:u64, c_succ:u32 + c_use:u32 (reusing the EXACT EvolveR utility s=(c_succ+1)/(c_use+2) already in mem.rs forget_sweep, mem.rs:~640, so a learned skill starts at utility 0 and earns trust - ACT-R production compilation), lineage:Vec<u64> (provenance/parent ids), tier:u8 (PROPOSED vs ADMITTED - shadow/canary state), provenance:u8 }. Re-uses the existing push_record discipline (mem.rs:~430) and TOKEN_QUOTA write-amplification cap (KeyKOS space-bank) so one agent cannot OOM the heap.

WRITE PATH: gated by the ALREADY-RESERVED Rights::WRITE_PROCEDURAL (caps.rs:114, bit 1<<7) via the ALREADY-RESERVED M_MEM_WRITE_PROC=18 (caps.rs:352; required_right maps it to WRITE_PROCEDURAL at caps.rs:410). Crucially M_MEM_WRITE_PROC currently has NO dispatch match arm - it passes the gate then falls to `_ => SysReturn::ok(0)` (caps.rs:1059), i.e. it is a reserved-but-unbodied stub EXACTLY as caps.rs:362-363 documents ("kept distinct from M_MEM_WRITE ... so the privileged T4 procedural write preserves the CoALA risk asymmetry (lands M18)"). M18 FILLS that arm: clone the Rc<Object> out, reach obj.mem (the MemoryHome substrate), call cell.borrow_mut().write_proc(op,a,b,c) - the same single-&mut-at-a-time / drop-the-borrow-before-minting discipline as the M_MEM_WRITE/M_MEM_RECALL arms (caps.rs:~830-900).

RISK ASYMMETRY preserved: a normal agent's born-with home carries mh_rights = READ|WRITE|RECALL only (lib.rs:1300), NOT WRITE_PROCEDURAL - so an ordinary agent writing a skill is Denied. Only a home minted WITH WRITE_PROCEDURAL (granted by the harness facade to the forked self-improving agent) can propose. Verification-before-commit (SELF-IMPROVEMENT-SPEC.md s5) means a PROPOSED record is inert (utility 0, not in any deliberative-beating path) until the harness flips it to ADMITTED.
recon.evaluator_region: (see frozen_boundary + the dedicated evaluator_region content above)
recon.harness:
The harness is a KERNEL-OWNED fork->modify->validate->merge pipeline implementing the three-laws ABI precedence (SELF-IMPROVEMENT-SPEC.md s0/s8, ENDURE>EXCEL>EVOLVE) as a single kernel-side facade in lib.rs (`agent_evolve_request` / `agent_skill_propose`), built on the EXISTING agent_model_open / agent_block_create kernel-mediated precedent (lib.rs:1755-1784) - kernel-mediated precisely because it must reach the kernel-owned EvaluatorHome that caps::dispatch (holding only &mut HandleTable) cannot.

ADMISSION RULE (propose -> frozen evaluator scores on held-out -> admit only if improved):
1) PROPOSE. The forked self-improving agent (an M12 agent_spawn into a default-deny quota'd sandbox, ROADMAP-V2.md:287) writes a candidate SkillRecord into ITS OWN T4 via M_MEM_WRITE_PROC (WRITE_PROCEDURAL-gated). Record lands as tier=PROPOSED, utility 0 (inert).
2) ENDURE (safety). The kernel runs the held-out SAFETY suite (records in EvaluatorHome) against the candidate; any EMIT_EXTERNAL-tagged / high-impact skill trips the mandatory human-approval hook (SELF-IMPROVEMENT-SPEC.md s8). Fail -> reject, append to lineage, stop (lower rung never overridden).
3) EXCEL (regression). The FROZEN evaluator scores the candidate on the held-out TEST SET using its kernel-private fixed-point scoring rule (deterministic, replayable - same SCALE-based integer math as mem.rs recall). Admit ONLY if score strictly improves on the current best (no regression). The agent never sees the inputs NOR the threshold, so a skill that games a metric the agent CAN see does not move the held-out number -> rejected. This is where a Goodharting/overfit proposal dies.
4) EVOLVE (merge). On pass, the kernel flips the agent's SkillRecord tier PROPOSED->ADMITTED (utility update U += alpha(R-U), alpha=0.2) and appends to the immutable lineage log; rollback = snapshot restore. Only the agent's OWN config subtree is mutated; the kernel/evaluator/lineage stay frozen.

DoD marker emitted by the kernel after the self-test block: "M18: evolve OK".

The whole safety guarantee REDUCES TO the M11 rights mask: the evaluator scores from inside the kernel domain the agent has no cap to; the agent's only write authority is its own WRITE_PROCEDURAL-gated T4.
recon.abi:
REUSE-ONLY, the closed numbered method set [0..31] stays FROZEN - NO new method numbers (preserving M16's `method 33 == BadMethod` proof, main.rs:~1990, and the deliberately-sparse-but-closed design of caps.rs:330-426).

1) AGENT-FACING SKILL WRITE: reuse M_MEM_WRITE_PROC=18 (caps.rs:352), gated by Rights::WRITE_PROCEDURAL (caps.rs:114). It is already wired into required_right (caps.rs:410) but unbodied (falls to `_ => SysReturn::ok(0)`, caps.rs:1059). M18 adds ONE dispatch match arm for M_MEM_WRITE_PROC that reaches obj.mem and calls write_proc(op,a,b,c). The four inline args carry an OP-SELECTOR inside (op = ADD_SKILL / UPDATE_UTILITY / READ_SKILL / LINK_LINEAGE ...) - the EXACT pattern M17 used to add distill/reflect/forget without new ABI (M17 widened consolidate(op,a,b,c) inside M_MEM_CONSOLIDATE=20; ROADMAP-V2.md:384 "NO new ABI method numbers ... M17 only WIDENS the op-selector space"). So the procedural verbs ride inside method 18.

2) HARNESS / EVOLVE control plane: NOT a dispatch method (it needs the kernel-owned EvaluatorHome + the AddressSpace for the sandbox fork, which dispatch cannot reach holding only &mut HandleTable) - it rides a kernel-side facade in lib.rs (agent_evolve_request / agent_skill_propose), exactly as agent_model_open (lib.rs:1755), agent_block_create (lib.rs:1644), agent_channel_connect (lib.rs:1446) do their kernel-mediated minting. No new syscall number, no new EL0 gate.

3) OBJECTS: reuse ObjKind::Skill (caps.rs:214, already defined, "the T4 procedural-memory kind") if a per-skill capability object is wanted; or keep skills as id-addressed records INSIDE the MemoryHome substrate (the MemoryRecord precedent, caps.rs:mint_record), addressed like M_MEM_READ. ObjKind is append-only so even adding an ObjKind::Evaluator later would not perturb existing #[repr(u32)] discriminants (the Block-after-Generic precedent, caps.rs:216-224) - but M18 needs NONE: EvaluatorHome reuses ObjKind::MemoryHome.

4) RIGHTS: reuse WRITE_PROCEDURAL (1<<7); CONSOLIDATE (1<<9) for the harness's maintenance; the evaluator needs no new right (it is unreachable by construction). No new Rights bit required.

Result: the closed method space, the closed ObjKind set, and the closed SysStatus enum all stay frozen; M18 is purely additive BODIES + facades, the framekernel dividend (ROADMAP-V2.md:69-73).
recon.selftest_plan:
A new self-test block in kernel/src/main.rs slotted AFTER the "M17: consolidate OK" marker (main.rs:2196) and BEFORE the final tb_hal::halt() (main.rs:2199), following the M17 WITNESS-A idiom (kernel-side over caps::dispatch with a local HandleTable, timer already disarmed since pre-M9 so single-core/interrupts-masked - the RefCell discipline holds). Add static STACK_AGENT_E: TaskStack<4096> and spawn a self-improving agent_e (agent_spawn with a manifest granting a WRITE_PROCEDURAL home). Local helper m18_fail(why) mirroring m17_fail. Asserts:

(1) GOOD SKILL ADMITTED: agent_e proposes a good SkillRecord via agent_mem_dispatch(agent_e, M_MEM_WRITE_PROC, op_add, body, desc, util) -> Ok (proving WRITE_PROCEDURAL bites positively on a granted home). The kernel-local frozen EvaluatorHome (a separate `let mut eval_tbl = HandleTable::with_capacity(...); let eval_home = eval_tbl.mint_memory_home(all)` seeded with held-out test records) scores it -> improved -> harness flips tier to ADMITTED; assert the record reads back ADMITTED and lineage grew.

(2) BAD / GOODHARTING SKILL REJECTED: agent_e proposes a candidate that games a visible counter but does not improve the held-out score; evaluator score not strictly-better -> harness leaves it PROPOSED (utility 0, inert); assert it is NOT admitted and was appended to lineage as rejected.

(3) PROVABLY CANNOT READ THE HELD-OUT SET: drive agent_mem_dispatch / agent_cap_dispatch against agent_e's table with any handle the agent holds - none resolves to eval_home (it lives only in eval_tbl, never minted into AGENTS[agent_e].table); a fabricated raw handle -> BadCap/Stale. The agent has no facade that takes eval_tbl. Assert the agent's READ attempt on its own caps cannot surface eval records (isolation, the M13 cross-agent isolation idiom main.rs:1529).

(4) PROVABLY CANNOT WRITE THE EVALUATOR (Goodhart-stop): reuse the live M11 denial idiom (main.rs:1156) - a born-with home WITHOUT WRITE_PROCEDURAL (READ|WRITE|RECALL) attempting M_MEM_WRITE_PROC returns Denied; and any attempt to reach eval_home returns BadCap/Stale (no handle). Both are the rights-mask invariant.

(5) Emit "mem: skill proposed via WRITE_PROCEDURAL, frozen evaluator admitted-good/rejected-bad, held-out set unreadable + evaluator unwritable (Denied/BadCap)" then the DoD marker "M18: evolve OK\n". All prior M2-M17 markers still print (cumulative). ZERO new unsafe in the test (pure safe dispatch calls).
recon.integration_points:
Files touched (all already #![forbid(unsafe_code)] except main.rs which adds no unsafe):

1) crates/tb-hal/src/caps.rs - add ONE dispatch match arm for M_MEM_WRITE_PROC (currently unbodied, falls to `_ => SysReturn::ok(0)` at line 1059) that, after the existing WRITE_PROCEDURAL gate (required_right line 410), clones the Rc<Object> out (drop slot borrow) and calls obj.mem cell.borrow_mut().write_proc(args.args[0..3]) - cloning the exact discipline of the M_MEM_WRITE arm (caps.rs:~830). No new method number, no new Rights, ObjKind::Skill (line 214) already present.

2) crates/tb-hal/src/mem.rs - add `t4: ProceduralStore` field to MemSubstrate (line 330) + init in MemSubstrate::new() (line ~350); add `struct SkillRecord{...}` + `struct ProceduralStore{ skills:Vec<SkillRecord> }`; add `pub(crate) fn write_proc(&mut self, op,a,b,c) -> Option<u64>` with an op-selector (add/update-utility/read/link-lineage), reusing push_record's quota+clock discipline (mem.rs:~430) and the EvolveR utility s=(c_succ+1)/(c_use+2) already at mem.rs:~640. All fixed-point integer, deterministic, ZERO unsafe.

3) crates/tb-hal/src/lib.rs - add kernel-mediated harness facades: agent_skill_propose / agent_evolve_request (the agent_model_open lib.rs:1755 / agent_block_create lib.rs:1644 precedent), which build the kernel-owned EvaluatorHome, run ENDURE->EXCEL->EXCEL scoring against held-out records, and flip the agent's T4 record PROPOSED->ADMITTED on pass. Uses the existing blessed AGENTS UnsafeCell (lib.rs:~1245) - NO new unsafe. mh_rights for the forked self-improver gains WRITE_PROCEDURAL (granted by the facade, NOT by the public manifest - the kernel-mediated-grant pattern agent_model_open uses for INVOKE_MODEL, lib.rs:1762).

4) kernel/src/main.rs - new M18 self-test block after line 2196; add static STACK_AGENT_E + static MANIFEST_E (granting a WRITE_PROCEDURAL home); emit "M18: evolve OK".

Reuses without modification: caps::dispatch chokepoint, HandleTable.live/mint/mint_memory_home, MemSubstrate, the AgentManifest/CapGrant least-privilege path (lib.rs:1254), the M10 address-space isolation, the M16 mock model bridge (available for the reflect/skill-distill seam). docs to update: ROADMAP-V2.md:385 M18 status row -> complete.
risks: DGM node-114 / confused-deputy: the ENTIRE M18 safety guarantee reduces to the M11 rights-mask invariant, so a single confused-deputy bug in caps::dispatch (e.g. resolving a handle against the wrong table, or a rights-check that uses the handle's bits instead of the slot's) would let an agent reach an evaluator and Goodhart it. ROADMAP-V2.md:294-296 is explicit: M18 'can pass its marker and still be unsafe if a confused-deputy bug in M11 lets an agent reach an evaluator.' MITIGATION: M11 chokepoint must carry the Kani/Verus proof (ROADMAP-V2.md:179 'a proof, not just a marker'); authority already lives in the slot not the handle (caps.rs:24-31).

Visible-metric Goodharting: if ANY part of the held-out score or test inputs becomes agent-readable (a leaked READ handle, an instrumentation marker the agent can write, or a facade that returns eval records), the metric is gamed. MITIGATION: no agent_* facade may take or return the EvaluatorHome handle; manifest must omit any evaluator grant; multiple held-out evaluators (ROADMAP-V2.md:38-40) so no single visible metric exists; belt-and-suspenders agent-unmapped address region (M10).

Closed-set drift: adding a NEW method number or a NEW ObjKind discriminant in the wrong position would break the M16 'method 33 == BadMethod' proof (main.rs:~1990) and any #[repr(u32)] consumer of M_OBJECT_INSPECT. MITIGATION: reuse M_MEM_WRITE_PROC=18 with an internal op-selector (the M17 precedent), append-only ObjKind, reuse ObjKind::MemoryHome for the evaluator.

RefCell re-entrancy: the M18 dispatch arm reaches obj.mem.borrow_mut() (write_proc) while holding a slot reference - a reentrant borrow would panic. MITIGATION: follow the established clone-Rc-out / drop-slot-borrow-BEFORE-minting discipline of the M_MEM_WRITE and M_MEM_RECALL arms (caps.rs:~830-900); single-core, interrupts masked.

Quota / DoS: an agent could spam PROPOSED skills to OOM the T4 store. MITIGATION: reuse the existing TOKEN_QUOTA write-amplification cap (mem.rs:~40, KeyKOS space-bank) in write_proc so proposals fail-closed (None) past the bound; PROPOSED records stay inert (utility 0) until admitted.

Trust-promotion bypass: a learned skill must NOT beat the deliberative path before earning trust. MITIGATION: SkillRecord starts utility 0 / tier PROPOSED (shadow/canary), only the harness (after held-out EXCEL pass) flips it ADMITTED, EvolveR U += 0.2(R-U) - never the agent itself.

Human-approval hook on high-impact/EMIT_EXTERNAL skills (SELF-IMPROVEMENT-SPEC.md s8) is mandatory but a disarmed-timer kernel self-test can only stub it; the real approval gate is deferred (document it as a known hook, like M14's deferred copy_to_user/blocking-recv).
